title: Youth Enrichment Programs * Immigrants - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Youth Enrichment Programs * Immigrants 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community events such as youth soccer, open gym, tutoring, community-specific workshops (topics include traditions, art, music, history, or language), leadership workshops, and clothing donation drives. Also assists in enrollment in the Oregon Health Plan (OHP) and helps individuals who are job searching, resume building, and with job application form completion. Provides information about rent assistance programs. 7535 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 972133905 Eligibility: Varies by service with a focus on African refugees and immigrant families Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Events: Varies by service Email (971) 430-6193 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION MULTI-COUNTY OFFICE UPLIFT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring and academic support by helping youth and families navigate complex academic systems. Also provides leadership training, culturally-specific mentorship, and athletics. 2110 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are immigrants or refugees Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 254-8916 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE UPLIFT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides tutoring and academic support by helping youth and families navigate complex academic systems. Also provides leadership training, culturally-specific mentorship, and athletics. 6835 SW Clinton Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Ages 9-26 who are immigrants or refugees Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday by appointment only Email (971) 254-8916 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES YOUTH & FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides educational classes, including English as a Second Language (ESL) and literacy classes. After school and summer youth enrichment programs with a focus on connecting with community resources, hearing from presenters on topics of interest, engaging in volunteer service activities, and gaining skills important for the future, such as resume building, scholarship writing, job searching, personal financial management, and navigating the health system. Child care is provided for ESL and literacy classes 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Varies with a primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Classes and youth enrichment: No standard hours Phone: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8:30am-1:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION AFRICAN REFUGEE IMMIGRANT ORGANIZATION AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION UPLIFT AFRICAN YOUTH AND COMMUNITY ORGANIZATION UPLIFT PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD YOUTH & FAMILIES
